title: Autograd, Seen
tagline: What autodiff is, what it is not, and how gradients move through a graph
summary: A visual explainer for automatic differentiation that first distinguishes autodiff from finite differences and symbolic algebra, then walks a single computation graph forward and backward before showing modules and mini-batch training.
highlights: Start with a clean comparison of finite differences, symbolic differentiation, and autodiff before touching the interactive graph.
See a single scalar graph reveal values on the forward pass and gradients on the backward pass, one stage at a time.
Keep the main graph clean while a separate ledger explains the exact upstream-times-local rule driving the current step.
Switch between atomic operations and framework-style modules to see what PyTorch or JAX are really hiding.
Explore a tiny three-example batch where the same parameters receive gradient contributions from multiple examples.
